title: On a Pincushion
description: On a Pincushion — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "On a Pincushion" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "A Toy Princess"
memes.description: "The story of a princess replaced by a mechanical doll, and the kingdom that forgot what a real princess was like."

memes.label: A Toy Princess
memes.description: A tale of a princess rescued from a stifling court by a fairy godmother who creates a magical mechanical double to rule in her place while the real princess grows up with love (004-020)

memes.label: Age
memes.description: Age and aging as markers of time, change, and wisdom in De Morgan's tales — from the old elf's elder status to the reflection that ages with its owner, and the vast fairy-tale timescales that dwarf individual lives

memes.label: Alys
memes.description: A young girl in "Siegfrid and Handa," the "greatest friend" of Lisbeth, who also buys the cheap magic shoes from the wicked gnome's stall (002-010).

memes.label: Beauty
memes.description: A double-edged theme in De Morgan's tales — physical beauty as both gift and curse, explored through Lamorna's loss and the Sunbeam's hidden nature

memes.label: Blanchelys
memes.description: A gentle, patient orphan girl in "The Seeds of Love" who wins a Prince's love through the magic of the rose tree seeds — counterpart to the jealous Zaire

memes.label: Bracelets
memes.description: A pair of chattering bracelets hanging over the looking-glass in the frame story of "On a Pincushion," representing the gossip and chatter of the wider world beyond the Pincushion (001-005).

memes.label: Bullfinch
memes.description: A grumpy bird character in the frame story of "On a Pincushion," whose nest becomes the setting for the Sunbeam and Moonbeam's tragic romance (001-221).

memes.label: Chancellor
memes.description: An elderly court official in "A Toy Princess" who, along with the Prime Minister, questions the fairy Taboret's revelation and ultimately votes for the sham Princess over the real one (004-074).

memes.label: charm
memes.description: Magical objects and enchantments that confer power, protection, or supernatural properties, appearing across multiple tales in the collection (002-028).

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Production notes, cover art credits, and revision history for this Standard Ebooks edition."

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: A recurrent moral virtue in De Morgan's tales — the quality of facing fear to act rightly, demonstrated by Blanchelys facing the yew-tree, Siegfrid facing the giant, and Lamorna facing her loss of beauty

memes.label: Courage vs. Magic
memes.description: A comparative analysis of earned versus magical heroism in On a Pincushion — Siegfrid's active courage in "Siegfrid and Handa" contrasted with the passive magical rescues in other tales

memes.label: Cruelty
memes.description: Cruelty in De Morgan's tales — the active choice to cause suffering, whether through abandonment, institutional punishment, or magical transformation

memes.label: Darkness
memes.description: Darkness as a liminal space of trial and transformation in De Morgan's tales — a passage to be traversed, never a place to remain

memes.label: Mary De Morgan
memes.description: Mary De Morgan (1850–1907), Victorian fairy-tale author of On a Pincushion (1877) — her narrative techniques, framing devices, and place in the Victorian fairy-tale tradition alongside contemporaries such as George MacDonald, Juliana Horatia Ewing, and Oscar Wilde

memes.label: Mary De Morgan's Narrative Techniques
memes.description: An analysis of Mary De Morgan's distinctive narrative techniques — frame narrative, miniature societies as moral commentators, gentle correction vs punishment, and the domestication of fairy-tale heroism

memes.label: Death
memes.description: Death as a threshold and transformative force in De Morgan's tales — rarely final, always meaningful

memes.label: Dream
memes.description: Dreams as narrative devices and psychological portals in De Morgan's tales — blurring the line between vision and reality

memes.label: Erick
memes.description: A kind-hearted young fisherman in "The Story of Vain Lamorna" whose steadfast love for the vain Lamorna is rewarded after the water people cure her of self-obsession

memes.label: Fairies
memes.description: Redirect to fairy — supernatural beings in De Morgan's tales.

memes.label: fairy
memes.description: Supernatural beings appearing throughout the tales, ranging from benevolent fairy godmothers to wicked enchantresses and elemental spirits (001-127).

memes.label: Fairy Godmother
memes.description: A benevolent magical guardian figure in "A Toy Princess" — Taboret, the fairy godmother who intervenes to protect Princess Ursula from a stifling court upbringing

memes.label: Fate
memes.description: Fate as resignation in De Morgan's tales — the moment when struggle yields to acceptance at the edge of doom

memes.label: Fear
memes.description: Fear as a universal experience in De Morgan's tales — the test that separates the brave from the paralyzed

memes.label: Fire
memes.description: The most physically present elemental force in On a Pincushion — a dual-natured power that destroys enchanted boots to liberate captives, tests endurance through sustained flame, and marks magical beings with sparks

memes.label: Fire-Fairy
memes.description: A fire elemental mannikin who guides Jack to Fireland and aids his quest in Mary De Morgan's "Through the Fire" (005-008).

memes.label: Fireland
memes.description: The underground realm of fire in "Through the Fire," home to fire-fairies and Princess Pyra, reached by journeying through the heart of a coal fire (005-058).

memes.label: Forgetting
memes.description: Forgetting and remembering as moral acts in De Morgan's tales — to forget is to fail, to remember is to honor

memes.label: Gratitude
memes.description: Gratitude as the proper response to kindness in De Morgan's moral universe — its presence heals and its absence condemns, structuring the moral architecture of tales from "The Hair Tree" to "On a Pincushion"

memes.label: Grief
memes.description: Grief as a measure of love and a catalyst for action in De Morgan's tales — never passive, always transformative

memes.label: Hair Tree Seeds
memes.description: Magical seeds from the Hair Tree that can restore lost hair — the central quest object in the eponymous tale, carried by the hero into a dangerous magical realm
aliases: magic seeds of the hair tree

memes.label: Handa
memes.description: The foundling girl in "Siegfrid and Handa," raised alongside Siegfrid, who wears the magic shoes and is rescued from the giant's captivity

memes.label: Hope
memes.description: Hope as a fragile but essential force in De Morgan's tales — sustaining characters through darkness and uncertainty

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "Standard Ebooks imprint and edition information for On a Pincushion."

memes.label: Jack
memes.description: The young everyman hero of "Through the Fire" — a seven-year-old crippled boy living alone in a London tenement who travels through the realms of fire and ice to rescue a star-crossed Princess and her water-Prince lover

memes.label: Jealousy
memes.description: Jealousy as the darkest emotional force in De Morgan's tales — driving destruction, theft, and attempted murder

memes.label: Kindness
memes.description: Kindness as the rarest and most quietly transformative virtue in De Morgan's tales — offered without expectation and repaid across generations

memes.label: Lamorna
memes.description: A vain but ultimately redeemable farmer's daughter in "The Story of Vain Lamorna" — whose obsession with her own beauty is cured by the magical intervention of the water people

relations.label: reflection stolen by the water people

memes.label: Lamorna Admits Her Fault
memes.description: The moral turning point of "The Story of Vain Lamorna" — Lamorna acknowledges that vanity caused her suffering, the moment of self-knowledge that enables her cure

memes.label: Life
memes.description: Life as a gift to be fought for in De Morgan's tales — never merely given, always requiring action and sacrifice

memes.label: Light
memes.description: Light in De Morgan's tales — the mark of hope, magic, and transformation that appears at moments of transition, whether as candle flame, sunbeam, or moonbeam

memes.label: Lisbeth
memes.description: The baker's daughter in "Siegfrid and Handa," the first villager to buy shoes from the wicked gnome's stall, whose purchase triggers a chain reaction that brings calamity to the village (002-010).

memes.label: List of Characters
memes.description: Complete index of all characters appearing across the tales in On a Pincushion and Other Fairy Tales.

memes.label: List of Concepts
memes.description: Complete index of all magical items, themes, and motifs across the tales in On a Pincushion and Other Fairy Tales.

memes.label: List of Events
memes.description: Complete index of all key plot events across the tales in On a Pincushion and Other Fairy Tales.

memes.label: List of Places
memes.description: Complete index of all locations across the tales in On a Pincushion and Other Fairy Tales.

memes.label: List of Tales
memes.description: Complete index of all individual tale entries in the On a Pincushion and Other Fairy Tales wiki.

memes.label: Little Jack
memes.description: A seven-year-old crippled boy, protagonist of "Through the Fire," who journeys to Fireland and the North Pole to help Princess Pyra and Prince Fluvius (005-001).

memes.label: Love
memes.description: The central relational force across all tales in On a Pincushion — appearing as romantic devotion, marital fidelity, parental love, and the transformative power that breaks enchantments and heals vanity

memes.label: Magic
memes.description: Magic as a narrative force in Mary De Morgan's tales — embedded in objects that test character rather than simply granting wishes

memes.label: Magic Candles
memes.description: Wish-granting candles given by a fairy — a magical item appearing in the framing narrative of "On a Pincushion" that drives the embedded tale of Siegfrid and Handa

memes.label: Magic Shoes
memes.description: Enchanted shoes made by a wicked shoemaker (a disguised giant) that compel the wearer to dance — a magical item of control and liberation in the tale of Siegfrid and Handa

memes.label: Memory
memes.description: Memory as a moral act in De Morgan's tales — to remember is to honor, to keep love alive, and to preserve the story beyond its moment. The Nightingale's nightly song, Jack's return to the fire-people, and the pincushion objects' remembered pasts all affirm memory as the collection's deepest value

memes.label: Mercy
memes.description: Mercy as supplication in De Morgan's tales — a plea made at the feet of power, its granting or refusal a measure of character

memes.label: Natural vs. Artificial
memes.description: A comparative analysis of natural and artificial spaces in On a Pincushion — how De Morgan uses setting to encode moral and emotional meaning, contrasting woodland and water settings with constructed environments of court and commerce

memes.label: Natural and Artificial Spaces
memes.description: How De Morgan uses natural and constructed spaces as opposing moral frameworks — natural settings as sites of transformation and healing, constructed spaces as sites of deception and captivity

memes.label: North Pole
memes.description: The far northern realm of ice where the old wise man dwells, visited by Little Jack on his quest for Princess Pyra (005-136).

memes.label: "On a Pincushion"
memes.description: "The frame story in which a Brooch, a Shawl-pin, and a Pin on a pincushion tell tales to pass the time."

memes.label: Owl
memes.description: The chief judge of the forest birds in "On a Pincushion" and "Siegfrid and Handa," a mercenary creature who demands Siegfrid's right eye as payment for his help (001-249, 002-033).

memes.label: Patience
memes.description: Patience as a virtue demanded by De Morgan's magical beings — the quality that earns the right to be heard and ultimately shapes fate

memes.label: Power
memes.description: Power as a relationship of domination and resistance in De Morgan's tales — exercised by the magical over the mortal, and always contested

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: Pride as a moral spectrum in De Morgan's tales — from proper self-respect to destructive arrogance

memes.label: The Prime Minister
memes.description: A courtier in "A Toy Princess" who, like the rest of the court council, preferred the artificial toy Princess over the real Princess Ursula (004-073, 004-109).

memes.label: Prince Fluvius
memes.description: The water Prince and son of the water King — who falls in love with the fire Princess Pyra across the elemental divide, risking being dried up for the chance of union with her (005-034)

memes.label: Princess Pyra
memes.description: The fire Princess and daughter of the fire King and Queen — the most beautiful woman in Fireland, who falls in love with the water Prince Fluvius across elemental boundaries (005-034)

memes.label: Princess Ursula
memes.description: The true princess in "A Toy Princess" — raised in loving obscurity by her fairy godmother Taboret while a magical Toy Princess takes her place at court

memes.label: Ralph
memes.description: The honest village shoemaker in "Siegfrid and Handa," father of Siegfrid, whose livelihood is destroyed when a wicked gnome undercuts him with cheap magic shoes (002-001).

memes.label: Reflection
memes.description: The reflection as a metaphor for self-regard, identity, and moral transformation — stolen from Lamorna to cure her vanity, aging with its owner as a record of lived experience, and replaced by the toy princess as a false image of royalty

memes.label: Rupert
memes.description: The young sailor hero of "The Hair Tree" — a questing everyman who journeys to a magical country to find the seeds of the legendary Hair Tree, confronting a golden bird, a talking tigress, and a snake-princess to restore his lost hair and win a bride

memes.label: seagull
memes.description: Redirect to the-seagull — the enchanted bird from Siegfrid and Handa

memes.label: Servant
memes.description: Servants and service in De Morgan's tales — from mortal pageantry to elemental magic, a role that bridges the human and the supernatural

memes.label: Shame
memes.description: Shame as a social regulator and moral signal in De Morgan's tales — teaching through discomfort rather than punishment

memes.label: Siegfrid
memes.description: The courageous hero of the eponymous tale "Siegfrid and Handa" — a young man who defeats a giant disguised as a shoemaker and rescues the captive children

memes.label: "Siegfrid and Handa"
memes.description: "The tale of little Siegfrid the shoemaker's son and Handa the miller's daughter, whose village was protected by an old proverb."

memes.label: Siegfrid and Handa
memes.description: The most traditionally heroic tale in On a Pincushion — Siegfrid, a shoemaker's son, rescues his beloved Handa from a giant disguised as a shoemaker by destroying enchanted boots with fire and repurposing the giant's own magic shoes for escape

memes.label: Siegfrid Enters the Workshop
memes.description: The moment Siegfrid enters the giant-shoemaker's workshop and discovers the captive children — the inciting event that launches the rescue

memes.label: spell
memes.description: Verbal or formulaic magical workings that can harm, protect, or enchant — a form of magic activated through speech or song (003-061).

memes.label: Spells and Charms
memes.description: The magical system of protective and offensive spells in De Morgan's tales — including counter-charms, spell shops, and sung enchantments that bind and protect

memes.label: Taboret
memes.description: The fairy godmother in "A Toy Princess" — a wise and caring magical being who secretly watches over Princess Ursula and crafts a Toy Princess to liberate her from a stifling court life

memes.label: The Barber
memes.description: The court barber in "The Hair Tree" — an executioner figure whose razor represents the threat of violence that the tale's justice system wields against women

memes.label: The Bridge
memes.description: The bridge over the great river in "On a Pincushion" — a threshold between the village and the castle, where fates are decided and lovers meet

memes.label: The Brooch
memes.description: A pebble brooch stuck in a pincushion who serves as the first storyteller in the frame narrative of On a Pincushion — a speaking object with a past life before being cut and polished (001-001)

memes.label: The Brook
memes.description: The stream running through Lamorna's village — a liminal space connecting the human world to the magical realm of the water people, and the site of Lamorna's transformation

memes.label: The Brook Grotto
memes.description: The special underwater grotto where the water elves keep Lamorna's stolen reflection — a jewel-box chamber beneath the brook

memes.label: The Burning of the Boots
memes.description: The climactic liberating event of "Siegfrid and Handa" — Siegfrid sets fire to the giant's enchanted boots in the underground workshop, destroying the instruments of magical captivity and freeing the imprisoned children

memes.label: The Castle
memes.description: The grand royal castle across the river — Blanchelys's eventual home as Queen, and the site of Princess Ursula's stifling court in "A Toy Princess"

memes.label: The Cottage
memes.description: The small cottage where Blanchelys and Zaire live, opening gates for passersby — the humble domestic setting from which Blanchelys rises to become Queen

memes.label: The Court
memes.description: The royal court — a space of protocol, performance, and social judgment, where a Toy Princess can pass for real and a real princess can feel invisible

memes.label: The Creation of the Toy Princess
memes.description: The magical act of creation in "A Toy Princess" — Taboret crafts a mechanical double of Princess Ursula so perfect that the court cannot tell the difference

memes.label: The Endurance Through Fire
memes.description: The climactic ordeal of "Through the Fire" — a Princess must sing continuously to survive flames, sustained only by a spell that requires unbroken performance

memes.label: The First Meeting at the Gate
memes.description: The first time the Prince stops at Blanchelys's cottage gate to ask for water — the human beginning of a love that the magic rose tree will later measure
aliases: the Prince stops at the cottage

memes.label: The Forest
memes.description: The dark forest bordering the village — the classic fairy-tale liminal space where heroes prove themselves, dangers lurk, and the boundary between the ordinary world and magical adventure dissolves

memes.label: The Frame Narrative
memes.description: The framing device of On a Pincushion — three pins on a pincushion tell stories to drown out chattering Bracelets, creating the collection's narrative structure

memes.label: The Giant Disguises Himself
memes.description: The off-stage event that sets "Siegfrid and Handa" in motion — a giant disguises himself as a shoemaker to kidnap children, creating the tale's central threat

memes.label: The Giant's Workshop
memes.description: The disguised workshop of the giant-shoemaker in "Siegfrid and Handa" — a trap for children made to look like an ordinary shop, where magic shoes are crafted and captives held underground

memes.label: "The Hair Tree"
memes.description: "A tale of a mysterious tree that grows human hair, and the consequences of vanity."

memes.label: The Hair Tree
memes.description: A standalone tale of Rupert's quest for magical Hair Tree seeds through an enchanted country of talking birds, transformed guardians, spell-shops, and a snake-princess — the collection's most fully realized magical ecosystem

memes.label: The Hare
memes.description: A talking hare who Siegfrid rescues from a trap in "Siegfrid and Handa" and who guides him to the underground cavern and the magic shoes (002-023)

memes.label: The Ice People
memes.description: A race of cold-dwelling magical beings in "Through the Fire" who freeze unwary travelers — counterparts to the benevolent water people, representing a hostile magical realm

memes.label: The Ice Realm
memes.description: The frozen territory of the ice people in "Through the Fire" — a lethal landscape where travelers can be frozen to death, representing nature at its most hostile

memes.label: The Island
memes.description: The mysterious island in "The Hair Tree" — covered with magical plants and reached only by those who dare to cross the river

memes.label: The Kiss on the Scar
memes.description: The emotional climax of "The Story of Vain Lamorna" — Erick kisses the scar on Lamorna's face, proving his love was never about her beauty and completing her moral transformation from vain girl to woman capable of receiving love

memes.label: The Magic Country
memes.description: The distant magical country in "The Hair Tree" where the Hair Tree grows — a realm of spells, transformed beings, and enchanted guardians

memes.label: The Miller
memes.description: Handa's father in "Siegfrid and Handa" — a flawed authority figure who admits wrongdoing and helps restore justice
aliases: the miller of Siegfrid and Handa

memes.label: The Moonbeam
memes.description: The moonbeam in De Morgan's "A Toy Princess" — a magical conduit between worlds through which the Princess appears and ultimately departs

memes.label: The Nightingale
memes.description: The Nightingale of "On a Pincushion" — singer, storyteller, and matchmaker whose song preserves memory across time

memes.label: The Wise Old Water Elf
memes.description: The elder of the water people in "The Story of Vain Lamorna" — a wise, courteous leader who devises the plan to steal Lamorna's reflection and is celebrated for his brilliant mind

memes.label: The Opal
memes.description: A magical gem from "On a Pincushion" — originally an ugly stone transformed into a brilliantly coloured Opal by the dying light of the Sunbeam and the Moonbeam, whose story is told by the Pin (001-289).

memes.label: The Palace Garden
memes.description: The garden at the royal palace where the rose tree grows and Princess Ursula walks — a cultivated space of both natural beauty and courtly constraint

memes.label: The Pin
memes.description: A common brass pin stuck deep in the pincushion — the most practical of the three complaining objects, who proposes storytelling as a way to drown out the noisy Bracelets

memes.label: The Pincushion
memes.description: The eponymous pincushion of the frame story — a miniature world where brooches, pins, and shawl-pins complain, tell stories, and moralize about human folly

memes.label: The Release of the Reflection
memes.description: The restorative event in "The Story of Vain Lamorna" — the water elves cut the sand ropes and release Lamorna's reflection back to her

memes.label: The River
memes.description: The great river in "On a Pincushion" and "The Hair Tree" — a boundary between worlds and a site of crossings, both literal and symbolic

memes.label: The Rose Tree
memes.description: A magical rose tree grown from jewel-like seeds that blooms in proportion to love — its health directly reflecting the strength of Blanchelys's marriage to the Prince

memes.label: The Sea
memes.description: The sea bordering the village in De Morgan's tales — Erick's fishing grounds, the water people's downstream neighbor, a working body of honest labor that contrasts with the magical inland brook

memes.label: The Seagull
memes.description: A magical seagull who aids Trevina in "The Story of Trevina" — messenger, comforter, and ally against the tortoise-king (003-079)

memes.label: The Seeds of Love
memes.description: Tiny jewel-like seeds from a rose that must be planted at sunrise — a magical item from the embedded tale "The Seeds of Love" in On a Pincushion

memes.label: The Seeds of Love
memes.description: The second embedded tale in On a Pincushion — the story of Blanchelys, who summons Love with a magic candle and plants jewel-like seeds that grow into a rose tree reflecting her marriage's health

memes.label: The Shawl-pin
memes.description: A jet shawl-pin stuck in the pincushion — a once-proud object fallen on hard times, who delivers the moral of "The Story of Vain Lamorna" with personal conviction

memes.label: The Shoemaker
memes.description: The old village shoemaker in "Siegfrid and Handa" — a wronged craftsman whose magical legacy redeems the community that abandoned him

memes.label: The Snake Princess
memes.description: An enchanted princess in "The Hair Tree" who appears as a snake — a guardian figure who must be dealt with through negotiation rather than combat

memes.label: The Spell Shop
memes.description: A shop "where every sort of spell or charm was sold" — a commercial magical establishment appearing in the tales, making magic accessible to ordinary purchasers

memes.label: The Story of the Opal
memes.description: The third embedded tale in "On a Pincushion" — a story of an opal that completes the Brooch/Pin/Shawl-pin storytelling session

memes.label: The Story of Vain Lamorna
memes.description: The first embedded tale in On a Pincushion, told by the Brooch — the story of a vain farmer's daughter whose reflection is stolen by water elves, curing her of self-obsession

memes.label: The Substitution Reveal
memes.description: The climactic revelation in "A Toy Princess" — Taboret reveals to the court that they have been ruled by a mechanical puppet, and the real princess returns

memes.label: The Summoning of Love
memes.description: The midnight scene where Blanchelys lights her magic candle under the yew-tree and summons Love himself — the transformative event that sets "The Seeds of Love" in motion

memes.label: The Suns Story
memes.description: A story within a story within a story — the Nightingale's tale of the Sun's son, embedded within "The Seeds of Love" within On a Pincushion

memes.label: The Sunbeam
memes.description: The lonely Sunbeam of "On a Pincushion" — a celestial being whose search for love and beauty drives the collection's most lyrical episode

memes.label: The Theft of the Reflection
memes.description: The pivotal magical event in "The Story of Vain Lamorna" — the water elves steal Lamorna's reflection from the brook, setting her on the path to cure her vanity

memes.label: the-tortoise-king
memes.description: Redirect to tortoise-king — the monstrous king of the tortoises from The Hair Tree

memes.label: the-tortoise-queen
memes.description: Redirect to tortoise-queen — the mother of the tortoise-king from The Hair Tree

memes.label: The Underground Cavern
memes.description: The underground prison beneath the giant's workshop where captured children are held — a place of darkness and captivity in "Siegfrid and Handa"

memes.label: The Village
memes.description: The rural village where Lamorna lives — a pastoral community centered on the brook, farming, and fishing, setting for "The Story of Vain Lamorna"

memes.label: the-water-people
memes.description: Redirect to water-people — a race of elf-like water beings from On a Pincushion

memes.label: The Water People's Festival
memes.description: The great festival the water elves hold after Lamorna repents and is cured — a communal celebration marking the successful conclusion of their moral intervention

memes.label: the-wise-old-elf
memes.description: Redirect to the-old-elf — the wise old water elf from The Story of Vain Lamorna

memes.label: The Yew Tree
memes.description: A great ancient yew-tree in the cottage garden whose branches almost touch the ground — the site where Blanchelys summons Love at midnight

memes.label: "Through the Fire"
memes.description: "A tale of young Jack who journeys to the North Pole and passes through fire to find the fire Princess."

memes.label: Through the Fire
memes.description: The collection's final tale — Jack braves the ice realm and witnesses a Princess's ordeal through fire, a story of endurance through elemental extremes

memes.label: The Tortoise King
memes.description: The antagonist of "The Story of Trevina" within "The Hair Tree" — a monstrous tortoise sovereign who abducts the heroine to his island kingdom, ruling alongside his spiteful mother the tortoise-queen

memes.label: The Tortoise Queen
memes.description: The tortoise-queen in "The Hair Tree" — the spiteful mother of the tortoise-king whose magical cruelty transforms Trevina into a tigress

memes.label: Toy Princess
memes.description: A magically crafted mechanical double of Princess Ursula, created by the fairy godmother Taboret to take the real princess's place at court while she is raised in loving obscurity

memes.label: Transformation
memes.description: A central magical motif in the tales whereby characters are physically changed in form — through curses, magic sugar, or fairy spells — often serving as trials or punishments (003-076).

memes.label: Trevina
memes.description: The youngest miller's daughter, protagonist of "The Story of Trevina" — kidnapped by the tortoise-king and transformed into a tigress (003-081)

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: Truth as a liberating but costly act in De Morgan's tales — spoken at great risk, bringing freedom and pain in equal measure

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Public domain dedication and copyright information for this ebook."

memes.label: Vanity
memes.description: A central moral theme in Mary De Morgan's "On a Pincushion" — the destructive self-absorption cured through magical intervention in "The Story of Vain Lamorna"

memes.label: Vanity Across Tales
memes.description: A comparative analysis of how Mary De Morgan treats the theme of vanity across two tales — the therapeutic cure in "Vain Lamorna" versus the substitution-based exposure in "A Toy Princess"

memes.label: Water-Fairy
memes.description: A water elemental messenger who carries word between Princess Pyra and Prince Fluvius in Mary De Morgan's "Through the Fire" (005-123).

memes.label: Water People
memes.description: Tiny benevolent elves who live beneath the water in brooks, never more than two or three inches high, who help worthy humans and are central to "The Story of Vain Lamorna"

memes.label: Wind-Fairy
memes.description: A wind elemental being who carries Jack through the air and guides him to the North Pole in Mary De Morgan's "Through the Fire" (005-153).

memes.label: Wisdom
memes.description: Wisdom as a celebrated communal virtue in De Morgan's tales — the quality of knowing the right course of action and guiding others toward it, appearing in the old elf, Taboret, Siegfrid, and the magic candles

memes.label: Zaire
memes.description: The jealous cousin of Blanchelys in "The Seeds of Love" — consumed by envy at Blanchelys's happiness, she serves as the tale's antagonist and moral counterpoint

memes.label: Zaire Questions the Tree
memes.description: The moment of psychological tension in "The Seeds of Love" — Zaire questions Blanchelys about the rose tree, her eyes glittering with malice
